what_it_does: Defines a design role registry: named element functions, their relationship strings, partial resolution pointers for colour/type/space/prominence, provenance, and confidence.
observations: Top-level meta explains the role layer, provenance, open status, cross-family resolution, and space-derivation rule.
Roles are keyed by names such as focal, title, subtitle, eyebrow, figure, label, marker, text, icon, heading, plate, inset, and member.
Each role entry uses is, in_relationships, resolves, provenance, and confidence fields.
Resolution values mix token pointers, grammar pointers, relationship-derived prose, and unresolved descriptive values.
The meta correction reframes label/figure-style couplets as containment plus slot/context semantics rather than serves/names semantics.
Extensibility and pending notes say the resolver does not yet read this file and typed edges/full cross-family resolutions are still future work.
standouts: Roles are described as functional identities independent of concrete resolved values.
The file records both authored vocabulary and intended resolver behavior in the same ledger.
The couplet-slot correction is explicitly captured as a semantic change.
Space is intended to derive from relationships instead of storing placements.
conventions: JSON object with meta, roles, _extensibility, and _pending sections.
Role keys are lowercase semantic labels.
Provenance uses bracket tags such as [DECK], [MOCKUP], [CONTENT], and [TIM].
Relationship and resolution fields are human-readable strings rather than typed objects.
Pointers use dotted paths into token and grammar namespaces when known.
concerns: The stated rule says resolves.space never stores px, but several space values include pixel ranges or pixel counts.
Relationship names are free-form strings, so direction, kind, and validation are not machine-explicit in this file.
Some resolves values are descriptive placeholders rather than pointers or relationship identifiers.
The correction says several couplet roles are pending resolver wiring, while current role entries still contain older-style relationship phrases such as introduces, points-at, and tags.
notes: This file is an open role ledger for design element functions and their intended cross-family resolution hooks.
questions: What exact structured form should replace the free-form in_relationships strings?
How should slot accepts/fills context be represented in role entries?
Which resolves values are intended to remain prose and which must become pointers or relationship identifiers?
Should pixel examples in resolves.space move to another field to preserve the derive-never-place rule?
purpose_vs_actual: The stated purpose matches the file's actual role-registry content, with local tension between the declared no-px space rule and several space entries that include pixel values.
intention_signals: It seems intended to preserve grounded design element identities while preparing them for a resolver that maps roles through relationships, dials, and context into concrete design values.
novelty: A design-token-adjacent role ledger that treats element roles as cross-family resolver inputs and records semantic corrections directly in metadata.
contribution: Contributes a neutral vocabulary of design roles, their intended relationships, provenance/confidence, and partial links to colour, type, space, and prominence values for a fused system to retain as role-layer substrate.
summary_for_embedding: This is a JSON registry of design element roles, with metadata, relationship strings, partial resolution pointers, provenance, confidence, and open notes for future resolver wiring.
intention_for_embedding: It was made to define grounded functional design roles that can later resolve across colour, type, and space through relationships, dials, and context.
